item.title: NEW Pixie & Brutus x Litterbox Crossover! [OC]
item.description:
The animated pilot is funded (THANK YOU!) but animation is very expensive, so we've lined up a bunch of exclusive rewards that will only be available by backing! Check them out here: https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/litterboxanimation/pilot-episode?ref=f52qpp
Big thanks to Ben for doing these collabs with me! They're always so fun! Be sure to follow him at https://www.instagram.com/pet_foolery/ for more brilliant comics!

item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: Happy Anniversary Honey
item.description: Void shout. My husband and I will be celebrating our ninth anniversary this month. Everything is good, except that we're both feeling a little sad because we have not had a night alone in seven years. Our son is autistic and needs significant support. He sleeps very poorly and only in very select conditions. We don't have anyone here who is qualified or close enough to him to watch him overnight. Because of this, we can't get away, even for one night. We haven't slept alone in our bed for years. For those without special needs kids, this may seem like a 'discipline' or 'training' issues. I promise it's not. Our son does not understand the concept of self soothing or staying in his own bed. He wakes up frequently and if he doesn't have one of us next to him to touch and cuddle and fall back to sleep, he wakes fully up for hours in the middle of the night. His soother is pinching our elbow skin - that's his blankie/stuffy that gets him back to sleep. We've gotten used to it, but we don't have anyone close enough that would fill in for such an odd sleeping arrangement. So, yeah. I guess when it comes down to it, it's a minor complaint. We're so lucky to have a great marriage, a healthy, enchanting little dude we worship, and the privilege of all being together at night. I'm not overlooking all that. But, sometimes, I really wish I could have a night just the two of us. Get a nice hotel. Fall asleep and wake up in each other's arms. It's been so long. Shout over. Thank you for reading. Y'all are the best.
item.dc:creator: hahway

item.title: August 8, 1982. A line drive foul ball hits a four-year-old boy in the head at Fenway. Jim Rice, realizing in a flash that it would take EMTs too long to arrive and cut through the crowd, sprang from the dugout and scooped up the boy.
item.description: August 8, 1982. - A line drive foul ball hits a four-year-old boy in the head at Fenway. Jim Rice, realizing in a flash that it would take EMTs too long to arrive and cut through the crowd, sprang from the dugout and scooped up the boy. He laid the boy gently on the dugout floor, where the Red Sox medical team began to treat him. When the boy reached the hospital 30 minutes later, doctors affirmed that Jim's quick actions unquestionably saved the boy's life. Jim returned to the game in a blood-stained uniform. A real badge of courage. After visiting the boy in the hospital and realizing the family was modest means, he stopped by the business office and instructed that the bill be sent to him. This is what a sports hero looks like! ----Copied-----
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: Why Postal Carriers Are In Love With Their Cartoonishly Ugly New Trucks - They have A/C Now!
item.description:
Soon, the iconic Grumman LLV will be allowed to retire and in its place will be the Oshkosh Defense NGDV. The USPS was sitting on a fleet of more than 135,000 Grumman LLVs and the youngest ones were 20 years old. The Grumman LLV was initially expected to service the postal service for 24 years, but these trucks have excelled in living up to their ‘Long Life Vehicle’ name. Prior to the LLV, the post office used to have existing vehicles modified for mail delivery duty, resulting in rides like the famous Jeep Dispatcher. The LLV was a shift from the USPS making an existing vehicle work to creating what it thought was the perfect mail truck for the era.
Even tall postal carriers can stand up without bonking their heads and walk from front to back to retrieve packages. For safety, the vehicles have airbags, 360-degree cameras, blind-spot monitoring, collision sensors and anti-lock brakes — all of which are missing on the Grummans. The new trucks also feature something common in most cars for more than six decades: air conditioning. And that’s key for drivers in the Deep South, the desert Southwest and other areas with scorching summers. “I promise you, it felt like heaven blowing in my face,” Stonum said of her first experience working in an air-conditioned truck. Others just love the fact that the new trucks don’t kill your back or break down from the AP: Richard Burton, another driver, said he appreciates the larger payload area, which can accommodate bigger packages, and the fact that he doesn’t have to crouch, helping him avoid back pain. The old trucks also had a habit of breaking down in traffic, he added. According to LLV operators, the machines are noisy, inefficient, and dangerously hot on sizzling days. As the AP notes, the LLVs are hot in other ways, too, such as the fact that nearly 100 of them caught fire last year alone. So, just about everyone wants them gone already. I asked my own letter carrier what he thinks about his LLV and he said, quote: “It’s slow and ridiculously hot.” Another bombshell landed in 2021 when Postmaster General Louis DeJoy said that only 10 percent of the NGDVs would be electric models because it would be too cost-prohibitive to buy more. This didn’t go over well with 16 states, six non-profit groups, a number of cities, the EPA, the Biden Administration, and more. Each pressured the USPS with public messaging andd through lawsuits to order more EVs. The 60,000 unit initial order eventually increased from 10 percent EVs to 20 percent, to 50 percent, and then finally, as Car and Driver reports, to 75 percent. By 2026, the USPS says all of its new NGDV orders will be electric.
https://www.theautopian.com/why-postal-carriers-are-in-love-with-their-cartoonishly-ugly-new-trucks/
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.title: I Stole This DUMP, So You Don't Have To. Tuesday Random Meme Dump of Pure Chaos
item.description:
1. Can't tell if this is a good thing or a bad thing :D
2. Imagine when he watches the Extended Editions too
3. The music makes it even more epic
4. A strong Darwin Award nominee: what could go wrong near a massive flood
5
6. How to fake a tequila shot
7. OVERTHINK
8. So that's who was driving the tank!
9
10. Smooth landing
11. What's your turtle review?
12. Horse rubber masks are so last decade
13
14. So apparently people will take whatever you hand them when talking on the phone...
15. Finally I would like to share with you that I'm working on a (hopefully) humorous game called Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse). And I'm trying to come up with wacky universes that the player can travel to. I already have the Upside Down Universe, The Chair Universe, The Universe Where Everything Is Dubbed in a Foreign Language and a few others. What else can I add? Do you have any suggestions for crazy Universes? Oh, and if you would like to support small indie devs, a simple wishlist on Steam, makes all the difference to us: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/

item.title: Kamakura, Japan
item.description: History: Kamakura was once the political capital of Japan, ruling from 1185 to 1333. It was the base of Japan's first feudal government. Temples and shrines: Kamakura has many temples and shrines, including some of Japan's oldest Zen temples. The architecture and character of the shrines reflect the influence of Zen, Nichiren, and Tendai Buddhism. Beaches: Kamakura has beaches that attract large crowds in the summer. Great Buddha of Kamakura: Kamakura is famous for its Great Buddha. Hato Sabure: This dove-shaped shortbread cookie is almost synonymous with Kamakura. The cookies symbolize peace and tranquility. Shirasu bowl: This is the most popular local cuisine in Kamakura. It's made with baby sardines, soy sauce, and other ingredients. Kamakura is also known for its hiking trails, literary heritage, and the Museum of Modern Art.
item.dc:creator: wolfwinter

item.title: #StandWithAnimation
item.description: Source:https://x.com/alexa_doodles/status/1835744551090155597 https://x.com/DiscussingFilm/status/1835716623128264787 Fuck corporations and fuck Disney, the demonization of artists has been demoralizing, from this to AI, those in power are attempting to take away human creativity and we must push back. Edit:https://www.ign.com/articles/inside-out-2-was-the-hit-pixar-needed-but-the-laid-off-employees-who-crunched-on-it-are-still-hurting

item.title: Trans Couple Tuesday
item.description:
Models: Icky & F1nn5ter https://www.instagram.com/big_icky/ https://www.instagram.com/f1nn5ter/ I adore these two so much. F1nn5ter's Instagram was removed for "copyright infringement", even though it's his account? He eventually just made a new one, that's why it's so smol. Cleary someone with an agenda was just abusing their power to remove his account, really sketchy. (F1nn5ter doesn't really call himself trans, but he says genderfluid is under the trans umbrella)
Tank!! (this is one of my favorite pictures on the internet)
Models: Alex Luxii & Michelle Alter https://www.instagram.com/alex_luxii/ https://www.instagram.com/michellexalter/ I wasn't 100% sure, but they recently confirmed in an AMA they are dating! So cute!
Models: Soapy Fate & Puppygirl W https://www.instagram.com/soapy_fate/ https://www.instagram.com/puppygirlw/ Not exactly a couple, I think they are poly!
Despite cosplaying Link a lot it seems like she's a Marth main based on some of her videos.

item.title: My youngest offspring took me to a Slipknot concert, I've been working a lot, she has school, volleyball, and a life, so we haven't spent much time together for a while. Sorry for the shitty video and sound, I'm like, 12 phones behind.
item.description:
25th Anniversary concert in Phoenix, she knows I'm a fan, and so is she. I lost my shit when she texted me, almost fell off the roof.
Sorry for the selfie, I was so excited and I don't really have friends to share stuff like this with . Anyway, make time for yourself and your loved ones, take care of yourselves, stay cool, stay hydrated, and most importantly, stay fresh cheese bags.
item.dc:creator: Jaqdakloun

item.title: So DNA was found on the murder weapon, not from the victim, not from the convicted and that tells you nothing??
item.description: Marcellus Williams is scheduled to be executed on Sept. 24 for a crime DNA proves he did not commit. The St. Louis County prosecuting attorney reviewed these DNA results and filed a motion to vacate Mr. Williams’ conviction because he believed the DNA results proved by clear and convincing evidence that Mr. Williams did not commit this crime. Although the circuit court has not yet scheduled a hearing to address this motion — and no court has ever considered the new exculpatory evidence — the Missouri Supreme Court set an execution date for Mr. Williams. That's seriously fucked up! https://innocenceproject.org/court-denies-prosecutors-motion-to-vacate-marcellus-williams-conviction-and-death-sentence/ https://drive.google.com/file/d/1H_8gEp_1LgUzEsUAEEXg1643UJw88rja/view
item.dc:creator: nickontop

I need her
